Healthy.disease_description: The plant is healthy and shows no signs of disease or infection. Leaves are functioning normally and photosynthesis is not affected. A healthy potato plant typically has strong green foliage, proper growth, and resistance to common pathogens when maintained under good conditions.
symptoms: No visible spots, lesions, or discoloration
Leaves appear firm, fresh, and well-shaped
Healthy.current_status: Plant looks healthy. No visible disease symptoms.
what_to_do: Keep proper spacing between plants for airflow
Monitor leaves every few days for spots or yellowing
approximate_quantity: Use only preventive organic spray like neem oil 3–5 ml per liter of water
precautions: Remove any fallen or dead leaves from soil

Early Blight.disease_description: Early blight is a common fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. It usually starts on older leaves and spreads gradually, especially in warm and humid conditions. If not treated early, it can reduce plant vigor and yield by damaging leaf tissue and reducing photosynthesis over time.
symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings (target-like pattern)
Leaves may dry, curl, and fall off
Early Blight.current_status: Fungal infection has started. Brown spots with rings may spread if untreated.
what_to_do: Start fungicide spray as soon as possible
Repeat spray every 7–10 days if symptoms continue
approximate_quantity: Option 1: Mancozeb — Around 2 to 2.5 grams per liter of water
Option 2: Copper based fungicide — Around 2 grams per liter of water
Spray until leaves are evenly wet, not dripping.
precautions: Spray in early morning or late evening
Wear gloves and mask while spraying
Stop spraying a few days before harvesting

Late Blight.disease_description: Late blight is a highly destructive disease caused by Phytophthora infestans. It spreads very rapidly under cool and humid conditions and can destroy entire crops in a short time. Unlike early blight, it affects both leaves and stems aggressively and requires immediate action to control.
symptoms: Rapid spreading of brown or black lesions
White fungal growth on underside in humid weather
Late Blight.current_status: Serious and fast spreading disease. Leaves may show dark, wet looking patches.
what_to_do: Remove and destroy badly infected plants or leaves
Spray again after 5–7 days if weather is cool and humid
approximate_quantity: Option 1: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb mix — About 2 to 2.5 grams per liter of water
Option 2: Cymoxanil based fungicide — Around 3 grams per liter of water
Ensure full coverage on both sides of leaves.
precautions: Do not mix different fungicides unless label says safe
Keep children and animals away from treated area
Avoid working in the field until spray has dried
